channel.title: Easy African Cuisine
channel.description: Authentic African recipes, easy to make at home.

item.title: Chicken Yassa (Authentic Senegalese Recipe, 30 min)
item.description: Senegalese chicken yassa: marinated in lemon and mustard, grilled, then simmered with deeply caramelized onions. Ready in 30 minutes.

item.title: Mafé (West African Peanut Stew with Chicken)
item.description: Mafé: West African peanut butter stew with chicken, tomato and root vegetables. A rich, creamy one-pot meal from Mali and Senegal.

item.title: Alloco (Ivorian Fried Plantains, Street Food Classic)
item.description: Alloco: ripe plantains fried until caramelized. The iconic Ivorian street food, ready in 15 minutes. Serve with spicy onion sauce.

item.title: Thiéboudienne (Senegalese National Dish, Rice & Fish)
item.description: Thiéboudienne: Senegal's national dish. Fish-stuffed and simmered with vegetables in a rich tomato sauce, served over red jollof-style rice.

item.title: Attiéké with Grilled fish
item.description: Attiéké with Grilled fish. Attiéké is the couscous of cassava Ivorian, served with of the grilled fish seasoned. A meal comp

item.title: Chicken Tagine with Preserved Lemon and Olives
item.description: Moroccan chicken tagine: slow-cooked chicken with preserved lemon, green olives, saffron and ginger. A fragrant North African classic.

item.title: Ndolé (Cameroonian National Dish with Bitter Leaves and Peanuts)
item.description: Ndolé: Cameroon's national dish. Bitter leaves simmered with ground peanuts, beef and shrimp. Rich, nutty, complex.

item.title: Ceebu Jen White (Rice with Fish Senegalese)
item.description: Recipe authentic of the ceebu jen white Senegalese : rice parfumé with broth of fish and vegetables fresh. A dish familial savory.

item.title: Dibi (Lamb Grilled Senegalese)
item.description: Recipe of the dibi Senegalese : lamb grilled spicy served with onions and mustard. A classique of the cuisine of street of Dakar.

item.title: Soup Kandia (Soup of Okra Senegalese)
item.description: Soup kandia Senegalese : sauce with okra, fish fumé and palm oil. A dish onctueux and savory à serve with of the rice.

item.title: Cameroonian Grilled Chicken (Poulet Braisé)
item.description: Poulet braisé: Cameroonian marinated grilled chicken with a tangy, spicy dipping sauce. Street food classic from Douala.

item.title: Egusi Soup (Nigerian Melon Seed Stew)
item.description: Egusi soup: Nigerian stew made with ground melon seeds, leafy greens, meat and palm oil. A thick, hearty West African classic.

item.title: Suya (Nigerian Spicy Peanut-Crusted Beef Skewers)
item.description: Suya: Nigerian grilled beef skewers crusted in spicy peanut rub (yaji). The ultimate West African street food, ready in 25 minutes.

item.title: Jollof Rice (Senegalese Thiéboudienne-Style Rice)
item.description: Wolof-style jollof rice: broken rice cooked in rich tomato-pepper sauce with smoky depth. The ancestor of all West African jollof.
